design_philosophy.core_loop: Select character → progress through linear missions → defeat aliens in side-scrolling combat → reach boss encounter → transition to shooting gallery or horizontal shooter segment → advance to next mission
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management of limited energy for special attacks; aliens hiding in environmental objects forcing constant vigilance
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Elite alien-hunting special forces operative eliminating an invading alien threat across escalating locations from streets to the alien mothership
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavy reliance on character selection determining playstyle (three distinct characters with unique weapons and special attacks); hybrid gameplay alternating between side-scrolling beat 'em up and shooting gallery/horizontal shooter segments; mission-based progression with environmental puzzle elements (finding hidden aliens in objects)

difficulty.checkpointing: Mission-based checkpointing; player restarts mission on failure
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character selection provides difficulty tuning via different weapon types and special attack mechanics; energy management creates strategic decisions about when to use powerful attacks

progression.upgrade_system: No permanent upgrades; power-ups obtained mid-mission (flying skulls provide life/energy restoration after defeating aliens)
progression.unlock_mechanism: All three characters available from start; eight sequential missions unlock as player advances through game
replayability_hooks: Three distinct playable characters with unique weapons and special attacks encourage replaying missions with different characters
Hidden aliens embedded in environmental objects reward thorough exploration and different strategies on replay
Difficulty scaling across missions creates reason to master earlier stages before advancing

world_building_techniques: Environmental progression from Earth streets to alien mothership establishes escalating threat
Character roster (special forces team named 'Alien Busters' with three distinct operatives) establishes setting through selection menu
Mission briefings define objectives (rescue people, destroy UFO) connecting gameplay to narrative goals
Boss encounters (alien spaceship, alien brain, alien queen) serve as narrative anchors for progression

boss_design.philosophy: Few boss encounters; emphasize pattern recognition and survival; one mid-game boss with three distinct forms appears again as common enemy near game's end
notable_bosses: Multi-form alien boss with three distinct phases (repeated as common enemy late game)

faction_indicators: Player choice between three 'Alien Busters' operatives (Karen, Garth, Scooter) establishes team identity
Unified goal of defending Earth from homicidal alien race creates in-group/out-group dynamic
danger_signals: Alien invasion threat serves as existential danger framing
Escalating difficulty across missions reflects growing alien threat intensity
Aliens hiding in mundane objects (plants, mailboxes, trash cans) creates paranoia/suspicion mechanics
environmental_storytelling: Location progression from urban streets to alien mothership illustrates invasion scope
Environmental objects containing hidden aliens communicate alien infiltration threat
Mission objectives (rescue civilians, destroy UFO installations) embedded in level design

sources_used: Wikipedia - Alien Storm article (gameplay, plot, character roster, mission structure, boss design, release information, reception)
